pending.body: In `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings` (success_reconciliation.rs ~line 856), the dedup key for existing mappings is `(finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)` — a 2-tuple that omits `active_bead_id`. However, in `rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal` (service.rs ~line 2460), the identity key is `(active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)` — a 3-tuple that includes `active_bead_id`. This inconsistency means that if two different active beads have the same finding_summary mapped to the same target bead, the reconstruction dedup would suppress the second mapping even though they are logically distinct. The 2-tuple key in `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings` should be `(active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)` to match the canonical identity key used everywhere else.

Add run/attempt provenance to persisted planned-elsewhere mappings, or defer writing them until the run is definitively the visible/successful one. `PlannedElsewhereMapping` currently stores only bead/summary/target fields in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:731-741`; restart rounds eagerly persist those records in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:3084-3090`; and success reconciliation later reloads every mapping for the bead by `active_bead_id` alone in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:719-739` via `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2413-2469`. If a run is rolled back, abandoned, or replaced by a fresh retry for the same bead after a restart round recorded a planned-elsewhere finding, that stale mapping will still be re-verified and commented as if it belonged to the current successful run.

Do not write the new `planned_elsewhere_mapped` milestone journal event without a backward-compatible read path or migration plan. The feature adds `MilestoneEventType::PlannedElsewhereMapped` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:288-297` and starts persisting it in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2375-2386`, but milestone journal reads still deserialize the enum directly and treat unknown variants as `CorruptRecord` in `src/adapters/fs.rs:3064-3083`. Any rollback/downgrade to a pre-feature checkout will fail to read `journal.ndjson` once one of these events has been written.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 14 -> Plan and Implement
